Accomplishments

Goal: ATI Web Accessibility Campus Plan

Developed a process for the web accessibility subcommittee to inform the revision and coordinate the implementation of the annual ATI Web Accessibility Plan.

7.6-web
2024-2025-7.6-web Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-13)

Evidence Summary:

The SF State ATI has built a custom graph database application that contains the entirety of the ATI success indicator (KPI) system developed by the chancellors office. All ATI data can and will be tracked in this system. A review process is implemented allowing executive sponsors to directly review each KPI before approval.

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-15 - Daniel Fontaine

The ATI Knowledge Graph contains a review system that allows stakeholders to review each success indicator before submission.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Process] Administrative Review Process

A complete review process is now implemented within the ATI Annual Report Knowledge Graph. A "review" button is present for each success indicator which is available to select admin reviewers. Each SI must be manually checked and reviewed before submission to the CO.

Webpages:
  • SF State ATI Annual Report

Developed a process for the ATI Steering Committee to review, revise and approve the updated ATI Web Accessibility Plan.

7.5-web
2024-2025-7.5-web Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-13)

Evidence Summary:

The SF State ATI has built a custom graph database application that contains the entirety of the ATI success indicator (KPI) system developed by the chancellors office. All ATI data can and will be tracked in this system. A review process is implemented allowing executive sponsors to directly review each KPI before approval.

Webpages:

Goal: Communication Process

Developed a process to ensure that campus members involved in creating and/or maintaining web sites, designs, and/or applications (web or mobile) know who to contact for compliance training, assistance, resources and support.

6.7-web
2024-2025-6.7-web Defined

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-17)

Evidence Summary:

The ATI is well establish entity within the drupal community of practice and presents on accessibility regularly. However, the information on our access page is beginning to stale and will need a full refresh after it is moved from DPRC to ITS.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Alexis Cabrerra - Public Affairs/Communication Specialist - cabrera@sfsu.edu
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Formal review of 6.7-web Abandoned

Review all documentation for clarity

Abandonment Notes: Will revisit when new OHO site and web accessibility process is implemented.
📝 Notes (1)
2025-11-17 - Daniel Fontaine

All content editors with access to drupal after the OHO refresh must take a course that will include accessibility. Total editors will be limited to around 50.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] Drupal Footer Accessibility Page

The SF State website must have a link to an accessibility page from the footer.

Webpages:
  • Accessibility Statement

[Process] Web Accessibility Process

This is the primary process for web accessibility at SF State

Documents:
  • Web Accessibility Process Document V1 Admin Review Milestones Depreciated
Webpages:
  • Moving your Site to Production Checklist
  • Web Accessibility Standards
  • Web Accessibility Process for New Websites
Notes:
  • 2025-10-18: PopeTech has been adopted by the CO and will be available by the end of the year for SF State.
  • 2025-10-10: With the loss of our web accessibility coordinator and the OHO refresh, there is currently no staff member assigned to perform web accessibility tasks.

Developed a process that integrates web accessibility information into the on-boarding/orientation process for new staff.

6.4-web
2024-2025-6.4-web Defined

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-17)

Evidence Summary:

New Employee orientation does contain links to the DPRC and Access, but we should provide more directed information. A training program will be developed between SM&C, ITS and ATI to training all content editors before they have editing access in the new OHO drupal update.

👥 Persons Involved (3)
  • Alexis Cabrerra - Public Affairs/Communication Specialist - cabrera@sfsu.edu
  • Nancy Rovegno-Ganner - Asst. Director of Operations • H/R - Human Resources (New Employee Orientation) - ganner@sfsu.edu
  • Ingrid Williams - AVP Human Resources (Section 504 Coordinator) - icwilliams@sfsu.edu

Find appropriate contact to make sure information is in onboarding Completed

Who is the correct contact for this?

Completion Notes: Nancy provided evidence of accessibility information inserted into her orientation slides.
📝 Notes (1)
2025-11-17 - Daniel Fontaine

All content editors with access to drupal after the OHO refresh must take a course that will include accessibility. Total editors will be limited to around 50.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] New Employee Orientation

Accessibility guidance included in new employee orientation processes and onboarding

Documents:
  • Employee Orientation Screen Shot Admin Review Milestones
Messages:
  • No date: Hi Dan, I offer the new employee orientation presentations for new employees once per month. I have a slide for professional development, where I include “Accessibility Technology Initiative website includes how-to/training for websites, documents, social media and more”. If you have an alternate training I’m happy to include it, or if you have a slide ready with more options I’m happy to add it to my slide deck. The next new employee orientation is next week Wednesday, Nov 5th. Thank you, Nancy

Develop an ongoing general campus communication with executive support that promotes web accessibility awareness.

2024-10-07

💬 Messages (1)
No date - Daniel Fontaine

Hi Dan, I’m reaching out regarding your message to Chris Trudell about web accessibility in New Student Orientation. We currently have a short module in our pre-Orientation system that directs students to the DPRC for additional details. I’d be happy to expand this module to include content about web accessibility or any other topics you’d like students to learn about the DPRC. We can coordinate this by email if you’d like to just share materials, or we can set up a meeting! Please let me know what works best for you! Kind regards,

📁 Implementation Evidence

📁 Implementation Evidence

Developed a training process on accessibility for digital content: documents (word processor produced, spreadsheets, presentations, PDF), to include Section 508 standards and the campus monitoring process.

5.6-web
2024-2025-5.6-web Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-18)

Evidence Summary:

With Equidox, we now have a clear training process and tool in place. We are primarily focussing on PDFs with the DPAR, which is acceptable as PDFs are by far the largest contributor to poor accessibility of our campus website. Codifying the DPAR into a long standing process will be an important goal for 2026.

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Launch the Drupal PDF Accessibility Project Completed Campus Plan

Drupal PDF Accessibility Review (DPAR)

Completion Notes: The launch of the drupal pdf accessibility review has been a great success. We've developed a straightforward monitoring and scanning process for PDFs and a clear communication and training process to report inaccessible PDFs and find the correct person to remediate them.
📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-10 - Daniel Fontaine

The Drupal PDF accessibility review project will help raise awareness and point content creators to accessibility resources.

3.6-web
2024-2025-3.6-web Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-14)

Evidence Summary:

The DPAR (Drupal PDF Accessibility Review) provides clear monthly scan updates for PDFs on drupal. Reports are sent to content creators and they are told to use Equidox to fix issues. This project will be converted into a regular process after completion in April 2026.

ITS plans, likely in 2027 to include a PDF verification step when adding PDFs to drupal using veraPDF. Users should be directed to ATI to fix PDFs.

2024-10-07

DPRC remediates PDF files upon request. The DPRC has developed an internal scanning and accessibility review application that will be deployed in 25 to scan and notify owners of PDFs of their PDF's access violations. Editors can upload whatever they want.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-03 - Shawn Hicks

Under the authority of the Director of Web & Mobile applications, all new web & mobile development conducted within the Information Technology Services (ITS) organization includes ATI reviews of all user interfaces under construction prior to deployment. All project plans that incorporate user interfaces must include this step alongside QA testing to track compliance.

📋 Plans (2)
Make official the responsibility for web monitoring Not Started

Add ATI Accessibility Process to internal IT documentation Abandoned

This is part of the web ati process review. We need to review how Ed gets requiests for accessibility reviews. Currently by email. How is this email generated?

Abandonment Notes: Ed has retired from the university.
📝 Notes (2)
2024-10-01

Measures of success: History stored on shared drive and in ticketing system

2024-11-17 - Daniel Fontaine

Manual Testing Process: The DPRC (Disability Programs and Resource Center) uses the Website Manual Evaluation Summary (WMES) template to track accessibility for selected pages on a website or web application. Departments are recommended to manually test five pages that best represent the content of the site. Additional pages may be selected by the DPRC for further verification​ Scope of Manual Testing: Manual testing complements automated testing by addressing accessibility standards that cannot be covered by automated tools. It is a critical part of ensuring the overall accessibility of web content​ Assistance for Departments: Campus departments are encouraged to perform manual testing with assistance from the DPRC. Training and resources for manual evaluations are also provided as part of the accessibility process​

📁 Implementation Evidence

  • Shawn Hicks - Director of Web and Mobile Applications - shawnhicks@sfsu.edu
  • Alexis Cabrerra - Public Affairs/Communication Specialist - cabrera@sfsu.edu
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Verify Campus Accessibility Statement is Updated Completed

The SF State accessibility statement is updated.

📝 Notes (2)
2024-12-09 - Daniel Fontaine

Accessibility Statement is in the Drupal Template Footer

2024-11-27

All SF State websites and web applications are required to include a link to SF State's Accessibility Statement, affirming the commitment to accessibility and offering feedback options (page 7). While there is a general mention of feedback mechanisms and reporting via accessibility statements, details on the exact operational workflow for users reporting issues (e.g., timelines for acknowledgment and resolution beyond escalation) could be clearer. The document demonstrates a clear structure for addressing web accessibility through published statements and issue reporting mechanisms. However: There is room for improvement in specifying workflows and user-friendly processes. The feedback loop for informing individuals of resolutions requires more definition.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] Drupal Footer Accessibility Page

The SF State website must have a link to an accessibility page from the footer.

Webpages:
  • Accessibility Statement
[Process] Web Accessibility Process

This is the primary process for web accessibility at SF State

Documents:
  • Web Accessibility Process Document V1 Admin Review Milestones Depreciated
Webpages:
  • Moving your Site to Production Checklist
  • Web Accessibility Standards
  • Web Accessibility Process for New Websites
Notes:
  • 2025-10-18: PopeTech has been adopted by the CO and will be available by the end of the year for SF State.
  • 2025-10-10: With the loss of our web accessibility coordinator and the OHO refresh, there is currently no staff member assigned to perform web accessibility tasks.

Developed a process to conduct manual accessibility evaluations on digital content: documents (word processor produced, spreadsheets, presentations, PDFs).

1.11-web
2024-2025-1.11-web Defined

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-18)

Evidence Summary:

The Drupal PDF Accessibility Review is a mature project with nearly 150 active participants. It leverages Equidox and a custom scanning and reporitng solution to deliver monthly conformance reports to content managers for each drupal sub domain at SF State. The SF State PMO is currently managing to project with the goal to reach near to full WCAG 2.1 compliance of all public PDFs by April 2026. Once the project has completed, resources will be redireceted into a PDF conformance tracking service within the SF State or Bay Area Regional Network. The previous web accessibility process did not contain clear directive or procedures to manage document accessibily on drupal. In the past content managers could, and did send PDFs to the DPRC for remediation and outsourcing.

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (2)
Launch the Drupal PDF Accessibility Project Completed Campus Plan

Drupal PDF Accessibility Review (DPAR)

Completion Notes: The launch of the drupal pdf accessibility review has been a great success. We've developed a straightforward monitoring and scanning process for PDFs and a clear communication and training process to report inaccessible PDFs and find the correct person to remediate them.
Add PDF remediation Service in ServiceNow On Hold

Explore a Snow workflow for PDF accessibility. Create webform for submission. https://sfsu.service-now.com/sp?id=sc_cat_item_guide&table=sc_cat_item&sys_id=08309247dbe1105081fd16994b96199c We need conversion resources first.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-10-07

Users may send PDFs to DPRC for remediation and charge back. Web Accessibility Coordinator provides direct support. More work is needed to solidify this process

📁 Implementation Evidence

PopeTech will be configured in 2026

  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu

2025-10-09 - Daniel Fontaine

The web accessibility coordinator role is currently unfilled. No web accessibility tasks are officially assigned to any position description.

2024-12-04 - Daniel Fontaine

SMC will now own content on public facing sites for new OHO sites.

  • 2025-10-18: PopeTech has been adopted by the CO and will be available by the end of the year for SF State.

Developed a process to conduct manual evaluations on multimedia digital content (video and audio).

1.12-web
2024-2025-1.12-web Initiated

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-18)

Evidence Summary:

There is no consistent internal process. ATI and SMC are available to assist if a request comes up.

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-27

The web accessibility process document mentions multimedia content as part of the accessibility evaluation process but lacks a comprehensive, detailed process specifically for conducting manual evaluations on video and audio. Key elements such as specific evaluation techniques, tools, prioritization criteria, and multimedia-specific evaluator training are not fully developed We currently do not have a defined process.

🏆 All Accomplishments for Web

Accomplished: Locate coordinator for New Student Orientation

We established a new connection with Carson Willits, who we can now coordinate with to provide updated accessibility information.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-6.5-web, 2024-2025-6.5-web

Accomplished: Deploy Verbit Unlimited Integration with Canvas Studio

Testing successful. All users have access to verbit.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-5.11-ins

Accomplished: Verify P-card trainings include TAR process and accessibility information.

Successfully verified that P-Card and buyer training contain appropriate references to the accessible procurement process within BuyIT

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-5.2-pro, 2023-2024-5.2-pro

Accomplished: Verify if New Employee Orientation Contains reference to TAR accessibility process

This information is only indirectly linked via a slide in the new employee orientation.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-5.1-pro, 2024-2025-5.1-pro

Accomplished: Find appropriate contact to make sure information is in onboarding

Nancy provided evidence of accessibility information inserted into her orientation slides.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-6.4-web, 2024-2025-6.4-web

Accomplished: Interview CPAGE about their own accessibility process

We've collected all the internal documents from CPAGE. They are a part of the cpage accessibility proces. It is important to note that accessibility work is distributed accross campus.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-8.12-ins, 2024-2025-5.12-ins, 2024-2025-5.13-ins, 2024-2025-5.11-ins, 2024-2025-5.14-ins, 2024-2025-6.4-ins, 2023-2024-6.4-ins

Accomplishment derived from completed plan: The SF State accessibility statement is updated.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-1.19-web, 2023-2024-1.19-web

Accomplished: Formalize Procurement Plan Approval Process

Accomplishment derived from completed plan: Develop a clear system to present updated process documents and plans to the ATI committee for approval inside the ATIKG

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-9.1-pro, 2023-2024-9.1-pro

Accomplished: Identify responsible personnel to re-implement a timely textbook adoption policy.

Accomplishment derived from completed plan: New bookstore committee will be started in November 2025.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-1.1-ins, 2023-2024-1.1-ins

Goal: ATI Procurement Plan

Developed a process for the ATI Steering Committee to review, revise and approve the updated ATI Procurement Plan.

9.1-pro
2024-2025-9.1-pro Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-10-28)

Evidence Summary:

The SF State ATI has built a custom graph database application that contains the entirety of the ATI success indicator (KPI) system developed by the chancellors office. All ATI data can and will be tracked in this system. A review process is implemented allowing executive sponsors to directly review each KPI before approval.

👥 Persons Involved (3)
  • Nish Malik - AVP Information Technology (Executive Sponsor) - nish@sfsu.edu
  • Michelle Rito Anolin - Director, Accessible Technology Initiative and Project Management Office - mich@sfsu.edu
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Formalize Procurement Plan Approval Process Completed Key Plan

Develop a clear system to present updated process documents and plans to the ATI committee for approval inside the ATIKG

📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-15 - Daniel Fontaine

The ATI Knowledge Graph contains a review system that allows stakeholders to review each success indicator before submission.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Process] Administrative Review Process

A complete review process is now implemented within the ATI Annual Report Knowledge Graph. A "review" button is present for each success indicator which is available to select admin reviewers. Each SI must be manually checked and reviewed before submission to the CO.

Webpages:
  • SF State ATI Annual Report

Developed a process for the procurement subcommittee to inform the revision and coordinate the implementation of Annual Procurement Plan.

Messages:
  • No date: Hi Dan, I offer the new employee orientation presentations for new employees once per month. I have a slide for professional development, where I include “Accessibility Technology Initiative website includes how-to/training for websites, documents, social media and more”. If you have an alternate training I’m happy to include it, or if you have a slide ready with more options I’m happy to add it to my slide deck. The next new employee orientation is next week Wednesday, Nov 5th. Thank you, Nancy

BuyIT is SF State's new TAR system. These are public resources about BuyIT and TARs.

Webpages:

  • Introduction to BuyIT at San Francisco State University

Developed a training process for Buyers (procurement staff).

5.4-pro
2024-2025-5.4-pro Defined

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-12)

Evidence Summary:

While this training is well established, without hard-evidence stored on a drive we can't raise the value to Managed. Setting to defined with this note.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • David Chellia - Accounts Payable & Fiscal Services Manager - chelliah@sfsu.edu
  • Larry Major Lee - Buyer III (Conducts Buyer Training) - larrylee@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-16 - Daniel Fontaine

Buyer 3 reports that TAR information is included in buyer training but there are no materials to share.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Service] Buyer Training

Primary Training for Campus Buyers

Notes:
  • 2025-11-03: - TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology. - There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt. - TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket. - If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement. - If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check.
Messages:
  • No date: Hi Daniel, In addition to training for new buyers, I also provide monthly requisition training to requesters on campus who sign up. For both buyer and requester training, I do touch upon the TAR process. It is not quite as extensive or in-depth as the information you provided in the link though. When TAR is brought up in trainings, what I inform to buyers and requesters is: - TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology. - There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt. - TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket. - If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement. - If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check. The link you provided includes some very useful information. I may tweak my training a little to include some of it. This could be a page I share with people during the training. I hope this information is helpful for your report. Let me know if you need anything else.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Service] Buyer Training

Primary Training for Campus Buyers

Notes:
  • 2025-11-03: - TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology. - There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt. - TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket. - If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement. - If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check.
Messages:
  • No date: Hi Daniel, In addition to training for new buyers, I also provide monthly requisition training to requesters on campus who sign up. For both buyer and requester training, I do touch upon the TAR process. It is not quite as extensive or in-depth as the information you provided in the link though. When TAR is brought up in trainings, what I inform to buyers and requesters is: - TAR is required for any technology purchase, regardless of the dollar value. This includes physical/tangible technology and software technology. - There is a pre-approved list of items that are TAR exempt. - TAR process includes ATI, ITS, and DPRC portions. TAR may be part of the Procurement process for certain purchases, but Procurement is not involved in the in-depth review. We check to see what may be required within the ticket. - If the TAR ticket concludes that certain IT supplemental provisions are necessary, then Procurement will include those into the Service Agreement. - If we are unsure if something requires a TAR or not, then it is best to submit a TAR to check. The link you provided includes some very useful information. I may tweak my training a little to include some of it. This could be a page I share with people during the training. I hope this information is helpful for your report. Let me know if you need anything else.

[Process] SF State Accessible Technology Procurement Process

Primary process managing accessible procurement. Covers topics main topics: ICT Definitions, Objectives, TAR (old) process, methods of procurement, pre-approved ICT, VPAT overview, product impact levels, basic review procedure, EEAAPs and exemptions.

Documents:
  • SF State Accessible Technology Procurement Process Admin Review Milestones
  • Accessible Procurement Analyst Position Description Admin Review Milestones
Webpages:
  • Accessible Procurement Process
Notes:
  • 2025-10-10: The current accessible procurement process is under review and will be updated by EOY. It will include updates from the CO regarding the new TAAP process.
  • 2025-10-10: Process does not contain information about different bid types.

Guidance given to vendors who need help creating VPATs/TARS

Documents:
  • Vendor Accessibility Demonstration Requirements Admin Review Milestones
Webpages:
  • Vendor Accessibility Requirements
  • Vendor Accessibility Requirements

Goal: Equally Effective Alternative Access Plans (EEAAPs)

📋 Goal Plans (1)

Finalize the updated TAAP authoring workflow. In Progress

We made solid progress in 2025 updating the EEAAP process to the TAAP. A top priority for 2026 will be to codify this process in the updated V2 procurement process documentation, and run multiple tests.

Develop a process for providing equally effective alternate access. The process must outline when an Equally Effective Alternate Access Plan (EEAAP) is required, assigned roles and responsibilities, and distribution strategy.

4.6-pro
2024-2025-4.6-pro Managed

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-12)

Evidence Summary:

The TAAP authoring process is clearly established but will need further refinement and testing in 2026. Setting to Managed as there are no structural roadblocks to completing effective alternative access plans.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
  • Mantasha Lakdawala - Accessible Procurement Analyst (Accessible Procurement Analyst) - mlakdawa@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Finalize the updated TAAP authoring workflow. In Progress Campus Plan

We made solid progress in 2025 updating the EEAAP process to the TAAP. A top priority for 2026 will be to codify this process in the updated V2 procurement process documentation, and run multiple tests.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-26 - Daniel Fontaine

DPRC Program Manager drafts and oversees the EEAAP creation in collaboration with the requesting department.

[Process] SF State Accessible Technology Procurement Process

Primary process managing accessible procurement. Covers topics main topics: ICT Definitions, Objectives, TAR (old) process, methods of procurement, pre-approved ICT, VPAT overview, product impact levels, basic review procedure, EEAAPs and exemptions.

Documents:
  • SF State Accessible Technology Procurement Process Admin Review Milestones
  • Accessible Procurement Analyst Position Description Admin Review Milestones
Webpages:
  • Accessible Procurement Process
Notes:
  • 2025-10-10: The current accessible procurement process is under review and will be updated by EOY. It will include updates from the CO regarding the new TAAP process.
  • 2025-10-10: Process does not contain information about different bid types.

The EEAAP template that must be completed in order to process an EEAAP

Documents:
  • TAAP Template Admin Review Milestones
  • SF State EEAAP Admin Review Milestones Depreciated
Notes:
  • 2025-11-03: The EEAAP process and forms are rebranded to TAAP in 2025/2026.

Goal: Procurement Processes

Developed a process for validating accessibility conformance claims (ACRs, Roadmaps, Accessibility Statements, accessibility demonstrations, RFP responses, and testing documentation).

1.8-pro
2024-2025-1.8-pro Managed

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-12)

Evidence Summary:

This is the primary duty of the accessible procument analyst. Resources are clearly linked in the report below.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Mantasha Lakdawala - Accessible Procurement Analyst (Accessible Procurement Analyst) - mlakdawa@sfsu.edu
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (2)
2024-12-13 - Daniel Fontaine

While the procurement process document is well articulated, it has not been reviewed since 2020. SF State has introduced a new TAR system called BuyIT. The updated procurement process must take these changes into account. Set to defined until review is complete.

2024-11-26

The document outlines a comprehensive process for validating accessibility conformance claims (ACRs, Roadmaps, Accessibility Statements, etc.) and integrates these processes into broader accessibility and procurement frameworks.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Process] SF State Accessible Technology Procurement Process

Primary process managing accessible procurement. Covers topics main topics: ICT Definitions, Objectives, TAR (old) process, methods of procurement, pre-approved ICT, VPAT overview, product impact levels, basic review procedure, EEAAPs and exemptions.

Developed a process to evaluate Section 508 compliance for campus adoptions of procurements made by multiple campuses or at the system wide level (e.g., Master Enabling Agreements).

1.9-pro
2024-2025-1.9-pro Initiated

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-12)

Evidence Summary:

This work is likely to be centrally managed by the CO. Setting to Initiated because we currently don't check with other campuses in any established way before approving a purchase. However, this will likely change in 2026.

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (3)
2025-11-03 - Daniel Fontaine

The CO is starting a new coordination effort called the SPAN networ which will manage MEA and contracts for multiple campuses.

2024-12-13 - Daniel Fontaine

While the procurement process document is well articulated, it has not been reviewed since 2020. SF State has introduced a new TAR system called BuyIT. The updated procurement process must take these changes into account. Set to defined until review is complete.

2024-11-26 - Daniel Fontaine

The process is present and effectively addresses Section 508 compliance for campus procurement, including references to MEAs. However, it lacks specific coordination mechanisms and oversight for system-wide implementation, preventing it from being fully managed (Level 4).

📁 Implementation Evidence

Developed a process for procurements that require an evaluation of Section 508 compliance. The process must address requirements for both competitive and non-competitive bid procurements.

1.4-pro
2024-2025-1.4-pro Defined

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-12)

Evidence Summary:

While there is process documentation in place, there needs to be more done to either train the a accessible procurement analyst to do this work in support of the SATSC or include this work as part of the web accesssibility position description. I'm setting this to defined as more definintion is need for training and responsibility for this work.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
  • Mantasha Lakdawala - Accessible Procurement Analyst (Accessible Procurement Analyst) - mlakdawa@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (2)
2024-11-25 - Daniel Fontaine

The accessible procurement process document provides a clear and functional process for determining the level and complexity of Section 508 evaluation required for new ICT procurements and renewals. This is primarily done through the Technology Acquisition Review (TAR) process and the use of the impact matrix. While comprehensive, the process could further benefit from refinements in automation, timeliness, and clear differentiation of complexity levels in renewals.

2024-12-13 - Daniel Fontaine

While the procurement process document is well articulated, it has not been reviewed since 2020. SF State has introduced a new TAR system called BuyIT. The updated procurement process must take these changes into account. Set to defined until review is complete.

Goal: ATI Instructional Materials Accessibility Plan

Developed a process for the ATI Steering Committee to review, revise and approve the updated ATI Instructional Materials Plan.

9.2-ins
2024-2025-9.2-ins Established

✓ Admin Review Complete (2025-11-13)

Evidence Summary:

The SF State ATI has built a custom graph database application that contains the entirety of the ATI success indicator (KPI) system developed by the chancellors office. All ATI data can and will be tracked in this system. A review process is implemented allowing executive sponsors to directly review each KPI before approval.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Andrew Roderick - AVP Academic Technology (Executive Sponsor) - roderick@sfsu.edu
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-15 - Daniel Fontaine

The ATI Knowledge Graph contains a review system that allows stakeholders to review each success indicator before submission.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Process] Administrative Review Process

A complete review process is now implemented within the ATI Annual Report Knowledge Graph. A "review" button is present for each success indicator which is available to select admin reviewers. Each SI must be manually checked and reviewed before submission to the CO.

Webpages:
  • SF State ATI Annual Report

Developed a process for the instructional materials subcommittee to inform the revision and coordinate the implementation of the Annual Instructional Materials Plan.

Goal: Accessibility Requirements for Multimedia, Interactive Content and Emerging Instructional Technologies

Developed a process for creating, adopting, implementing, and remediating emerging instructional technologies.

5.14-ins
2024-2025-5.14-ins Initiated

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Angie Petty - Instructional Designer - apetty@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-05 - Angie Petty

An Excerpt from a PD page in the Excellence in Online Pedagogies Course- https://sfsu.instructure.com/courses/46763 Designing for an Online Course Adapted from: Quality Matters. (2023, October). Bridge to quality: A QM online course design guide basic edition. Retrieved from: https://qualitymatters.org/higher-ed-bridge-guide-basic When choosing and creating course content: 1 Prioritize academic integrity by clearly identifying the source material and adhering to copyright law. 2 Ensure content variety, including different types of materials beyond textbooks, reflecting current thought in the field. 3 Ensure easy accessibility of all materials, such as short videos with clear audio and closed captions, and readable text. 4 Proactively meet accessibility requirements for all content, focusing on already accessible materials and creating accessible content when necessary. Course materials should be designed to facilitate readability and minimize distractions. This involves breaking up large blocks of text, using headings for organization, and ensuring appropriate font size, type, formatting, and color contrast. Avoiding excessive use of color or purely decorative images is recommended to prevent cognitive distractions. Additionally, text alignment and use of multimedia controls should be optimized for accessibility and ease of use. For multimedia content, educators should prioritize good audio quality, shorter video length, and clear resolution to maintain visibility of playback controls. Interactive elements should allow learners to control motion and navigation features. Providing accessible text-based materials is crucial for supporting the success of all online learners and ensuring compliance with accessibility policies. Educators should use the Canvas Accessibility Checker tool to check the accessibility of text-based materials and to review the accessibility of publisher materials. For additional guidance please review the Disability Programs and Resource Center’s (DPRC) website: https://access.sfsu.edu/ Consider a holistic view of technology usage, avoiding reliance on a single tool and considering pedagogical fit when selecting technologies. Multimedia should be used effectively, with clear audio and non-distracting visual elements, and synchronous technologies should be accessible to all learners.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] CEETL Courses Containing Accessibility Guidance

These are CEETL courses that focus on faculty developement and instructional design. They should contain notes about accessible course design and other helpful guidance related to ins goal 4.

Webpages:
  • Designing for an Online Course
  • Renovate & Elevate in Canvas

Developed a process for creating, selecting, adopting, and remediating audio and video assets.

Completion Notes: Testing successful. All users have access to verbit.
Verify Audio and Video remediation is included in LMS Remediation Framework. On Hold

Verify Audio and Video remediation is included in LMS Remediation Framework.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-12 - Daniel Fontaine

The accessible procurement process will review basic VPAT but nothing beyond what is reported in the vpat. Remediating is handled ad hoc with AT and DPRC available to assist.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Process] Internal CPaGE Processes For Accessibility On Canvas

All of CPaGE documentation for accessibility. CPAGE mannages canvas courses.

Documents:
  • Job Aid: Checking Quiz Accessibility Admin Review Milestones
  • Job Aid: Narrating a PowerPoint with Audio and Video Admin Review Milestones
  • Job Aid: How to check for Accessibility in Canvas Admin Review Milestones
  • Job Aid: How to Remediate Files Admin Review Milestones
Notes:
  • 2025-10-27: No instruction is given to instructors about selecting course materials. They may be pointed to CEETL. Accessiblilty is a part of the course shell template creation. DPRC is referenced as well as Sensus Access. The CPAGE team goes for 85% accessibility. They use the built in accessibility checker in Canvas. Majority of videos have transcripts (spot checked). Student assistant will check for Tags and will do auto tags in PDF. Will check if slides are in the original powerpoint format.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-11-21 - Daniel Fontaine

8.12-ins
2024-2025-8.12-ins Defined

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Anoshua Chaudhuri - Senior Director CEETL - anoshua@sfsu.edu
  • Angie Petty - Instructional Designer - apetty@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Document AT/CEETL Proceedures for Course Accessibility. On Hold

To rasie to established, we need prove with documentation.

📝 Notes (2)
2025-11-13 - Daniel Fontaine

Through the JEDI Course, new faculty foundation, JEDI workshops on UDL 3.0 -AC

2024-12-05 - Angie Petty

All PD courses developed by AT, CEETL, and collaborative SMEs go through a rigorous development process that strives to meet all CSU, 508 and 504, and WCAG 2.2 standards to integrate accessibility information into faculty development. All image have appropriate ALT text, text size and contrast meet standards, all videos have at least auto-generated and reviewed closed captioning. Materials are appropriately chunked and are reviewed by multiple SMEs. Courses are facilitated and corrected as needed then goes through a feedback and revision process for reimplementation for future courses. All courses direct faculty to resources that include DPRC and the Longmore Institute and shares information with faculty about student resources such as librarians, DPRC, TASC, counseling services, etc. Course review and revisions are discussed in all courses.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] Public CEETL Guidance Regarding Disability and Accessibility

CEETL provides a central advocacy role for equity and this includes disability

These are CEETL courses that focus on faculty developement and instructional design. They should contain notes about accessible course design and other helpful guidance related to ins goal 4.

Webpages:
  • Designing for an Online Course
  • Renovate & Elevate in Canvas

Goal: Creation and Adoption of Accessible Instructional Materials

Developed a process that incorporates accessibility when acquiring, converting, digitizing, creating, and maintaining library assets.

7.11-ins
2024-2025-7.11-ins Defined

⏳ Admin Review Pending

Evidence Summary:

The library has a number of people who incorporate accessibility into their work as is shown in the documentation provided. However, we would need to conduct a more robust analysis of which assets the library actually has to determine if we are actually meeting our accessibility goals.

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Alex Cherian - Library Services Specialist (BATV) - acherian@sfsu.edu
  • Christy Stevens - Interim University Librarian - crstevens@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (3)
2024-12-05 - Christy Stevens

The library uploads electronic theses and dissertations to ScholarWorks, and we have workflows for ETDs that concentrate on accessibility: * https://docs.google.com/document/d/1abRkS8rH940YdKY74F5WHVJSML_7zMPvT8YVN08Q8Tw/edit?usp=sharing * https://docs.google.com/document/d/19pwWgundn-qob2fv9VE6k8k13Gr6VAcJCYJ10sN_k6Q/edit?usp=sharing We also created accessible templates, which Graduate Studies disseminates: Master's Thesis Template: https://sfsu.box.com/s/4n8llfw8umgeev0ayjmj7pkgoyvi4lz8 EDD Dissertation Template:https://sfsu.box.com/s/31433jr7vs2lxof5yogjuoblj29nfhm9

2024-12-05 - Christy Stevens

The Library's Electronic Resource Acquisitions Procedure document is available here: https://sfsu.box.com/s/3weu09bq982gapp478icupq6a36plwak Before we acquire a new database, the following criteria must be met:   1. Is the database based on IP authentication or SAML authentication?   2. If it is a full-text database, does the vendor provide records in Alma Community Zone? If not, do they provide MARC records for discovery?   3. Does the database provide 508 Compliance access?   4. Does the vendor have a VPAT?   5. Does the resource provide statistics, such as COUNTER statistics?   6. Will the vendor accept payment by purchase order (PO) and email the library an invoice?  7. If it is a new vendor, is the purchase just a single item?     The following criteria are optional, but it would be ideal to have them:  1. Can the resource use OCLC EZproxy?   2. Does the database support an OpenURL link resolver, specifically the Alma link resolver?   3. Does the resource have an administrative interface?   If the resource is streaming media, the following questions will need to be clarified as well. 1. Are the films captioned or do they have subtitles? 2. What format will the files be provided?  (For DSL) 3. Does the vendor provide MARC records for the films?  https://sfsu.app.box.com/file/1717835725895 Library databases are provided by multiple vendors, and accessibility information is provided through Voluntary Product Accessibility Templates (VPAT), the self-assessment template completed by a vendor to generate an Accessibility Conformance Report, providing information on how a vendor’s product conforms to Section 508 accessibility standards. VPATs for our databases are available here: https://sfsu.box.com/s/hfuw2kji5cxwx5w03iuobcay36lk5kv3

2024-12-05 - Christy Stevens

The Bay Area Television Archives Quartex web platform includes closed captions and transcript downloads for all 2226 clips currently streaming online. This offers an inclusive learning environment, supporting WCAG 2.1 AA access compliance. https://batv.quartexcollections.com/ We are developing a method of downloading existing transcript files from our Quartex video platform, converting them to WebVTT files and then uploading those files to video assets in DIVA. This produces closed captions in the DIVA videos. Below is an example of how this looks in DIVA: https://diva.sfsu.edu/collections/sfbatv/bundles/239336

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Guidance] General Guidance For Web Content Creation that Includes Accessibility

Any guidance about web content creation that includes accessibility that may not be directly linked to ATI.

Documents:
  • Writing for the SF State Library website: A checklist Admin Review Milestones
Webpages:
  • SFSU Branding Site
  • Website Content
[Process] BATV Captioning Process

SFSU Library captioning process for BATV archives

Documents:
  • Quartex Transcript to WebVTT to DIVA Admin Review Milestones
Messages:
  • No date: Hey Dan, Thanks for checking in. Please follow this link for relevant documentation. Progress is slower than expected, but we’ve finished about 650 of the TV Archive's 3000 streaming clips, And all new clips are being added with captioning. We think a realistic estimate is to finish about half of the remaining clips by the end of December 2025 and the rest by April-May 2026. We're also hoping to start on a separate project in 2026 to provide Black American Sign Language support for selected clips featuring our Black Panther Party Collection. But this is a whole different level of access and will take time to implement. I'll keep you updated as that takes shape. Please let me know if you have any questions. Regards, Alex
[Process] Accessibility Process for Electronic Theses and Dissertations

The library uploads electronic theses and dissertations to ScholarWorks, and we have workflows for ETDs that concentrate on accessibility

  • Ed.D. Dissertation Template Admin Review Milestones
  • Masters Thesis Templates Admin Review Milestones
  • Stage 2 ETD Processing: Acrobat Quality Control Admin Review Milestones
  • Stage 1 ETD Processing Admin Review Milestones

  • Library Database VPATS Admin Review Milestones
  • Electronic Resource Acquisitions Procedure Admin Review Milestones

[Service] Accessible Syllabus Tool

Academic Technology’s Syllabus tool allows faculty and academic office coordinators to build and manage class syllabi. The service draws content from campus databases and allows users to create, share, and re-use syllabi in ways that save time and allow for a paperless approach to managing this resource. Users can publish accessible versions in multiple formats including directly into iLearn courses.

Webpages:
  • Online Syllabus Tool

Campus has formally documented (through Policy or Resolution) faculty responsibility for selecting and authoring accessible instructional materials.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-04 - Daniel Fontaine

The DPRC uses a custom application to run daily scans of courses requesting accommodation. Alt Media Production staff have immediate and clear access to files needing remediation. Students can opt in to a "Daily Scan". DPRC will pull all files updated files and convert them on schedule. https://github.com/Fontaineconsult/canvas-bot-v2 this tool is only used for direct accommodations. It could be used for course review with additional resources.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-02

The campus course review and approval process is administered through the Division of Undergraduate Education. The process is initiated for all new or revised courses and refers to accessibility compliance requirements and links to the campus Course Accessibility Checklist (https://access.sfsu.edu/ati/content/sf-state-course-accessibility-checklist). https://ueap.sfsu.edu/curriculum-development/course-information/review-approval-process No link to accessibility information.

4.5-ins
2024-2025-4.5-ins Defined

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Daniel Fontaine - Senior Accessible Technology Services Coordinator (ATI Coordinator) - fontaine@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Courses listed for CEETL/AT need review Abandoned

Do they contain updated accessibility guidance?

Abandonment Notes: Duplicate
📝 Notes (2)
2024-12-04 - Daniel Fontaine

The DPRC uses a custom application to run daily scans of courses requesting accommodation. Alt Media Production staff have immediate and clear access to files needing remediation. Students can opt in to a "Daily Scan". DPRC will pull all files updated files and convert them on schedule. https://github.com/Fontaineconsult/canvas-bot-v2 this tool is only used for direct accommodations. It could be used for course review with additional resources.

2024-10-06

Accessible Media Program Coordinator and ATS Program Manager have administrator access to all courses in LMS support site. Access to this is granted a few days before the start of the Academic Year.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[Service] Canvas Tracking (Canvas-Bot)

The DPRC uses a direct API integration and custom software to monitor changes in instructional content and makes it accessible for students with print accommodations.

Webpages:
  • Canvas Bot Git Hub
[Guidance] CEETL Courses Containing Accessibility Guidance

These are CEETL courses that focus on faculty developement and instructional design. They should contain notes about accessible course design and other helpful guidance related to ins goal 4.

Webpages:
  • Designing for an Online Course
  • Renovate & Elevate in Canvas

  • Accessible Media Quick Converter

  • Angie Petty - Instructional Designer - apetty@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (2)
2025-10-21 - Daniel Fontaine

Users can now request professional captions directly in Canvas Studio. The DPRC continues to recommend Canvas Studio as the best video player to use for course content.

2024-12-05

I am not on the current AY's NFF Canvas page, but in the 2023 version NFF (2023) - https://sfsu.instructure.com/courses/22248 General Faculty Resources J. Paul Leonard Library Information for Faculty - https://library.sfsu.edu/information-faculty including information Course Reserves in the Library, Library Collections & Spaces, Media, and Interlibrary Loan

  • Document Accessibility

Developed a process to review the accessibility of faculty-maintained websites or web applications, whether hosted on the campus domain or elsewhere

4.11-ins
2024-2025-4.11-ins Initiated

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (2)
  • Andrew Roderick - AVP Academic Technology (Executive Sponsor) - roderick@sfsu.edu
  • Shawn Hicks - Director of Web and Mobile Applications - shawnhicks@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-03

Faculty web applications used for instruction (distinguished from faculty websites) are supported by Academic Technology (AT) which manages them and ensures their accessibility. Accessibility compliance is assessed at the time of procurement or acquisition. In some cases, additional reviews take place based on a specific case or accommodation which is facilitated between AT and the Disability Programs and Resource Center (DPRC). Faculty are free to select 3rd party online tools for the use in instruction which are not directly supported by AT but still require accessibility assessment and approval. While this approval will take place in cases where the tool is specifically procured, there are cases where a review may not take place (for example, publisher-based online tools provided as part of a package with textbook selection). The campus is examining options for ensuring the review and approval of these tools.

📁 Implementation Evidence

Goal: Timely Adoption

Develop a process (e.g., developed and documented practices, specified staff time, educational/training resources, and technology) to achieve compliance with timely adoption.

1.2-ins
2024-2025-1.2-ins Not Started

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Chris Farmer - Director of Operations - Ucorp (Bookstore Account) - cfarmer@sfsu.edu
📝 Notes (1)
2024-10-05

Responsibility for process lies with Associate Vice President for Academic Affairs Operations. Adoption numbers are tracked by SFSU Bookstore textbook manager. The Associate Vice President for Academic Affairs Operations position no longer exists. Re-establishing the process will align with 1.1-ins.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[InternalPolicy] Timely Textbook Adoption

Policy documents describing the timely textbook adoption policy

Webpages:
  • policy-timely-adoption-textbooks-students No Longer Exists
Messages:
  • No date: Hi Daniel, I hope you’ve been well. I wanted to reach out regarding our previous conversation around our campus’ approach to course material adoption compliance/reporting and your interest in joining UCorp’s Bookstore Advisory Committee. Brian Beatty, Bookstore Advisory Committee Chair, and I met this morning to plan for our Fall 2025 meeting, which we are hoping to have either early November or just before the Thanksgiving break. Before the committee meets, we need to finalize our roster, so I am reaching out to you to confirm your interest. We plan to focus on adoption compliance/reporting pertaining to accessibility and affordability. The goal is to find ways to improve and formalize the communication with faculty around the requirement each term. While the bookstore does all it can to contact faculty and follow up with those that are not in compliance, etc., we really need support from the campus to strengthen the message and importance of communicating selected materials (even if no materials are required) to our campus bookstore. Years ago, something like this existed that went to all faculty possibly from the Provost’s office, but it has not existed in quite a few years. I believe this will tie in well to the oversight you have been asked to take on and you’d make a great addition to the group. Please let me know and if you are interested, I will be in touch on how we add you officially to the committee. Best, Chris
  • No date:
  • No date: n partnership with the ATI, the Academic Senate passed the Timely Adoption of Textbooks for Students Policy (S08-249) in Spring 2008 that set textbook adoption deadlines for each academic year. Departments are required to submit orders for their course materials by these established deadlines in order to ensure access and compliance with this policy. B
Metrics:
  • LH_TEXT_ADOPT_2_1449900633:
  • Adoption Compliance Spring 2021 11-23:
  • Adoption Compliance Spring 2021 02-05-21:

Campus has formally documented (e.g., Policy, Resolution, or Procedure) a process to ensure the timely adoption of textbooks and other instructional materials.

1.1-ins
2024-2025-1.1-ins Not Started

⏳ Admin Review Pending

👥 Persons Involved (1)
  • Chris Farmer - Director of Operations - Ucorp (Bookstore Account) - cfarmer@sfsu.edu
📋 Plans (1)
Identify responsible personnel to re-implement a timely textbook adoption policy. Completed Key Plan Campus Plan

New bookstore committee will be started in November 2025.

📝 Notes (1)
2024-12-11

The Bookstore Advisory Committee, which previously facilitated timely textbook adoptions, has been mostly inactive in recent years. It appears no one from the university administration, such as Academic Affairs, is currently sending official reminders to faculty about textbook adoption deadlines. While the bookstore partner Follett makes efforts to collect adoption information from faculty, they face challenges without more support and communication from the university. Changes in Follett personnel have also contributed to difficulties in the process. The ATI will reconnect with stake-holders in Q1 25.

📁 Implementation Evidence
[InternalPolicy] Timely Textbook Adoption

Policy documents describing the timely textbook adoption policy

Webpages:
  • policy-timely-adoption-textbooks-students No Longer Exists
Messages:
  • No date: Hi Daniel, I hope you’ve been well. I wanted to reach out regarding our previous conversation around our campus’ approach to course material adoption compliance/reporting and your interest in joining UCorp’s Bookstore Advisory Committee. Brian Beatty, Bookstore Advisory Committee Chair, and I met this morning to plan for our Fall 2025 meeting, which we are hoping to have either early November or just before the Thanksgiving break. Before the committee meets, we need to finalize our roster, so I am reaching out to you to confirm your interest. We plan to focus on adoption compliance/reporting pertaining to accessibility and affordability. The goal is to find ways to improve and formalize the communication with faculty around the requirement each term. While the bookstore does all it can to contact faculty and follow up with those that are not in compliance, etc., we really need support from the campus to strengthen the message and importance of communicating selected materials (even if no materials are required) to our campus bookstore. Years ago, something like this existed that went to all faculty possibly from the Provost’s office, but it has not existed in quite a few years. I believe this will tie in well to the oversight you have been asked to take on and you’d make a great addition to the group. Please let me know and if you are interested, I will be in touch on how we add you officially to the committee. Best, Chris
  • No date:
  • No date: n partnership with the ATI, the Academic Senate passed the Timely Adoption of Textbooks for Students Policy (S08-249) in Spring 2008 that set textbook adoption deadlines for each academic year. Departments are required to submit orders for their course materials by these established deadlines in order to ensure access and compliance with this policy. B
Metrics:
  • LH_TEXT_ADOPT_2_1449900633:
  • Adoption Compliance Spring 2021 11-23:
  • Adoption Compliance Spring 2021 02-05-21:

Develop a process to distribute timely adoption performance reports to campus administration each semester.

🏆 All Accomplishments for Instructional Materials

Accomplished: Locate coordinator for New Student Orientation

We established a new connection with Carson Willits, who we can now coordinate with to provide updated accessibility information.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-6.5-web, 2024-2025-6.5-web

Accomplished: Deploy Verbit Unlimited Integration with Canvas Studio

Testing successful. All users have access to verbit.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-5.11-ins

Accomplished: Verify P-card trainings include TAR process and accessibility information.

Successfully verified that P-Card and buyer training contain appropriate references to the accessible procurement process within BuyIT

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-5.2-pro, 2023-2024-5.2-pro

Accomplished: Verify if New Employee Orientation Contains reference to TAR accessibility process

This information is only indirectly linked via a slide in the new employee orientation.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-5.1-pro, 2024-2025-5.1-pro

Accomplished: Find appropriate contact to make sure information is in onboarding

Nancy provided evidence of accessibility information inserted into her orientation slides.

Advances YSE: 2023-2024-6.4-web, 2024-2025-6.4-web

Accomplished: Interview CPAGE about their own accessibility process

We've collected all the internal documents from CPAGE. They are a part of the cpage accessibility proces. It is important to note that accessibility work is distributed accross campus.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-8.12-ins, 2024-2025-5.12-ins, 2024-2025-5.13-ins, 2024-2025-5.11-ins, 2024-2025-5.14-ins, 2024-2025-6.4-ins, 2023-2024-6.4-ins

Accomplishment derived from completed plan: The SF State accessibility statement is updated.

Advances YSE: 2024-2025-1.19-web, 2023-2024-1.19-web

Accomplished: Formalize Procurement Plan Approval Process

Accomplished: Identify responsible personnel to re-implement a timely textbook adoption policy.
